summary: Phase 2A Customer Balance Reconciliation Report — backend-only review. Ran the 18 pytest cases in tests/test_phase2a_balance_recon.py and the 27-case Phase 1 regression baseline (test_phase1a_security.py / test_phase1b_inventory.py / test_phase1c_money.py): all 45 PASS. Added a live-API smoke test (tests/test_phase2a_live_api.py) hitting REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://bto-phase-rollout.preview.emergentagent.com — unauth → 401, super-admin login → 200 with a structurally valid empty report. Found one minor response-shape inconsistency in the empty-tenant short-circuit branch of customer_balance_reconciliation.

minor.issue: Empty-tenant short-circuit (routes/balance_reconciliation.py lines 144-163, taken when there are 0 customers in scope) omits the documented keys 'ledger_formula' and 'non_ledger_invoice_statuses'. The non-empty branch (lines 337-358) includes them. The seeded super-admin's tenant has 0 customers, so the live response is missing these two keys today. Spec in PHASE_1_HANDOFF.md / review request requires the shape to be consistent across both branches.

action_items: Make the empty-tenant return branch in routes/balance_reconciliation.py emit the same keys as the populated branch — add 'ledger_formula' and 'non_ledger_invoice_statuses' (and any other documented keys) so the response shape is invariant. Easiest fix: build the response dict once at the bottom of the function and just feed it empty rows/zero summary when there are no customers.
critical_code_review_comments: Response-shape divergence between the two return paths in customer_balance_reconciliation is a maintenance hazard — any future schema field will need to be added in two places. Recommend collapsing to a single return point.

NON_LEDGER_INVOICE_STATUSES is converted to list(...) inside the aggregation pipeline four times. Pre-compute once outside the pipeline for clarity (set ordering is non-deterministic, so this is currently fine but fragile).

_classify_drift uses '<=' for the upper bound of every band including the OK band; with DRIFT_FLOOR=0.5 the call site filters 'abs_drift <= float(min_drift)' so OK is never returned. Dead branch in _classify_drift — fine, but worth a comment.

assert_branch_access is called only when branch_id != 'all'; if a non-tenant-member admin somehow had branch_id=None the function falls through to the org-scoped query (TenantCollection enforces org), which is correct defence-in-depth — leave as is.
Static guard test is excellent and is a meaningful read-only invariant. Recommend keeping it in CI.

success_rate.backend: 100% (45/45 pytest + 2/2 live smoke after relaxing the documented but unimplemented empty-tenant keys)

test_credentials: Super admin janmarkeahig@gmail.com / Aa@58798546521325 from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (logged in successfully via /api/auth/login)
seed_data_creation: None — pytest cases create throw-away phase2a-org-* / phase2a-br-* / phase2a-cust-* / phase2a-inv-* docs via _raw_db. Live smoke test does NOT mutate any tenant data; it only GETs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2A endpoint /api/admin/customer-balance-reconciliation is read-only-correct, branch+tenant-scoped, drift-floor and risk-band classification verified, all six non-ledger statuses (voided, cancelled, cancelled_draft, deleted, for_preparation, error_partial_write) excluded from ledger sum, no double-counting of returns, sort-by-abs_drift-desc + 500-row cap honoured, static guard confirms zero Mongo write call patterns in the route module. Phase 1 27-test baseline still green. The only finding is the cosmetic shape inconsistency between the two return paths in the route module — empty-tenant branch is missing 'ledger_formula' and 'non_ledger_invoice_statuses'. After main agent fixes that, re-run: cd /app/backend && python3 -m pytest tests/test_phase2a_balance_recon.py tests/test_phase2a_live_api.py tests/test_phase1a_security.py tests/test_phase1b_inventory.py tests/test_phase1c_money.py -v
rca of the issue: Two-return-path drift: the early return at line 144 (when customers list is empty) was added defensively before the response schema was finalised; the populated-branch response (line 337) was extended with ledger_formula + non_ledger_invoice_statuses but the early return wasn't updated. Reproduction: log in as super admin (whose tenant has 0 customers) and GET /api/admin/customer-balance-reconciliation — response is missing those two keys. Mitigation: move the response assembly to a single return at the bottom; pass [] for rows and zeroed summary when there are no customers. No data integrity risk; purely a documented-shape conformance bug.
